featurePanels.title: Round Read
items: Round 15 is a major bye round with only five NRL games and seven clubs idle.
The strongest read is Warriors over Sharks because Cronulla are still without Nicho Hynes and have Origin/injury strain in the pack.
Dolphins over Roosters is the next-best lean on form, but both sides are reshuffled by representative duty.
Rabbitohs versus Broncos is a survival spot rather than a clean betting spot.

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Do not force a charity bet during a disrupted Origin-bye round.
Brisbane are desperate but missing Reece Walsh, Payne Haas and Kotoni Staggs.
The Roosters still have Daly Cherry-Evans and Angus Crichton, so the Dolphins tip is not a banker.
Tigers versus Titans is volatile after Penrith put 68 on Wests Tigers and Gold Coast stole the M1 derby late.

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Protect the positive Mark Hughes Foundation ledger at +$0.53.
Wait for final 24-hour teams before discussing any stake.
Treat Round 15 tips as lineup-driven reads, not normal-strength ratings.
Keep Round 14 as a tips review only because no Round 14 charity bet was locked.

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Warriors home side against a Sharks spine missing Nicho Hynes.
Dolphins outside backs with Jamayne Isaako, Jack Bostock and Herbie Farnworth against a depleted Roosters backline.
Raiders forward base against an Eels spine listed without Mitchell Moses.
Titans confidence after Keano Kini and Jayden Campbell broke the Broncos late.

betCard.title: Round 15 Charity Bet
betCard.kickoff: Round runs Thursday 11 June to Sunday 14 June 2026
why: The ledger is positive at +$0.53 and there is no need to chase a bet in an Origin-bye round.
Warriors H2H and Dolphins H2H are the only price-watch candidates from the current read.
Neither becomes a charity bet without a fresh TAB price, stake, match, and explicit user confirmation.
risks: Major Origin-bye rounds produce unusual lineups and late player movement.
Several favourites may be shorter than their disrupted team sheets justify.
Any new charity play needs recorded odds, stake, match, and final team context before kickoff.

Rounds 10 and 11: No charity bet locked, so the ledger stayed unchanged.
Round 12: No charity bet locked; tips went 5 from 5, but the ledger stayed unchanged.
Round 13: Penrith Panthers head to head at $1.62 won 20-18, returning $10.53 from a $6.50 stake.
Round 14: No charity bet was locked, so the ledger stayed unchanged.
After Round 14: Ledger is 3-2, $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.

watchlist: Price-watch Warriors over Sharks only if the market does not overreact to Cronulla's missing spine.
Price-watch Dolphins over Roosters, but respect the Roosters' remaining senior spine and edge power.
Do not bet Rabbitohs v Broncos or Tigers v Titans without a clear market edge.
Keep the $10.53 Round 13 collect separate from any new Round 15 decision.
Bulldogs, Sea Eagles, Storm, Knights, Cowboys, Panthers and Dragons are on the bye.
confidence: Warriors over Sharks - strongest raw read with Cronulla missing Nicho Hynes
Dolphins over Roosters - form lean, Roosters Origin depletion caveat
Raiders over Eels - pack and defensive edge against a thin Eels spine

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 15 is all about disciplined reads through Origin disruption
lines: As of Thursday, 11 June 2026, Round 15 team lists have been checked against current published reports.
Round 15 has five games: Rabbitohs v Broncos, Dolphins v Roosters, Warriors v Sharks, Eels v Raiders, and Wests Tigers v Titans.
The charity ledger remains 3-2 with +$0.53 settled profit because no Round 14 charity bet was locked.
Round 15 is live as a preview board only; no new charity bet is locked.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: The best bet is still patience unless the price is real
lines: The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger is 3-2 with $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.
Warriors over Sharks is the cleanest raw Round 15 tip, but it may be priced accordingly.
Dolphins over Roosters has form appeal, yet the Dolphins also lose Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow and the Roosters still carry Daly Cherry-Evans.
No Round 15 charity bet should be recorded without a fresh TAB price, stake and user confirmation.

sections.title: Round 15 Fixtures
sections.heading: Round 15 fixture board
lines: Rabbitohs v Broncos - Thursday 11 June, 7:50pm at Accor Stadium.
Dolphins v Roosters - Friday 12 June, 8:00pm at Suncorp Stadium.
Warriors v Sharks - Saturday 13 June, 5:30pm at Go Media Stadium.
Eels v Raiders - Saturday 13 June, 7:30pm at CommBank Stadium.
Wests Tigers v Titans - Sunday 14 June, 4:05pm at Leichhardt Oval.
Bulldogs, Sea Eagles, Storm, Knights, Cowboys, Panthers and Dragons have the bye.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who gets upgraded or held back this week
lines: Upgrade: Warriors at home, Dolphins form, Raiders pack base, and Titans confidence after the M1 comeback.
Hold: Rabbitohs because the home tribute night helps, but the backline is patched together.
Downgrade: Broncos without Walsh, Haas and Staggs; Sharks without Hynes; Tigers after the 68-0 Penrith loss.
Volatility bucket: Rabbitohs v Broncos and Tigers v Titans.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The Round 15 team-list details driving the card
lines: South Sydney name Jai Arrow in jersey 23 for a tribute night, while Campbell Graham and several Origin players are unavailable.
Brisbane list Hayze Perham at fullback with Reece Walsh, Payne Haas and Kotoni Staggs unavailable; Ezra Mam remains on the bench.
The Dolphins list Trai Fuller at fullback for Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow, while Isaiya Katoa is expected to be released from Blues camp.
The Roosters are without James Tedesco, Sam Walker and Mark Nawaqanitawase; Cody Ramsey starts at fullback.
Cronulla remain without Nicho Hynes, while the Warriors get the home-ground edge in Auckland.
